commit.message:
feat: refactor indexer as importable library

- Add public library API via src/lib/index.ts
- Export all services, types, and factory functions
- Configure package.json with exports field for library usage
- Add files field to control published files
- Add tsconfig.lib.json for library-specific build
- Create .npmignore to exclude source and dev files
- Refactor services to support both singleton and direct instantiation
- Make MetadataService constructor public
- Make ProcessorService accept options object
- Add BackfillConfig interface for configuration
- Update GeyserStreamingService with comprehensive JSDoc
- Add build:lib and build:all npm scripts
- Support dual-mode: standalone app and importable library

Library consumers can now:
import { GeyserStreamingService, MetadataService, ProcessorService } from 'solana-nft-indexer';
import type { Transaction, NFTMetadata } from 'solana-nft-indexer';

Add critic (basal ganglia): TD-weighted CE loss for plasticity modulation

TaskStateCritic evaluates task_state to predict performance. Surprising
outcomes (high TD error) get amplified CE gradient, expected outcomes
get attenuated. Critic never inputs to fire() — only shapes how much
to learn, not what to compute.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix video prompt duration: write actual seconds, replace placeholders

- Instruct Gemini to write real duration number (4/6/8) instead of
  [SHOT_DURATION_SEC] placeholder in video prompts
- Add server-side fallback to replace any leftover placeholders
- Snap duration to nearest valid Veo 3.1 value (4, 6, or 8)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix move grading: UCI score is from side-to-move, not always white

The centipawn loss formula was wrong. After the mover plays, the next
position's eval is from the *opponent's* perspective (UCI convention),
so it must be negated before subtracting:

  loss = cpBefore − (−cpAfter) = cpBefore + cpAfter

The previous code did cpBefore − cpAfter (or cpAfter − cpBefore for
black), which doubled the apparent loss on every move, classifying
normal book moves as inaccuracies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Model parameter to analysis/reasoning commands

Opus default: debug, review, troubleshoot
Sonnet default: doc, test, commit, iniClaude, pr

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Show pending plan change notice on billing page when downgrading

- Sync endpoint now checks Stripe subscription schedules for pending plan changes
- Billing page shows blue notice: "Your Business plan is active until [date].
  After that, you'll switch to the Starter plan."
- Plan row shows current → pending badge with arrow
- "Renews" label changes to "Switches On" when a plan change is scheduled
- Cancellation warning only shows when no pending plan (avoids double notice)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix repeated subscription/follow API calls on project reading page

Clear ?subscribed query param after detecting it to prevent retry loop
re-triggering on re-renders. Use apiToken instead of session object as
dependency for follow status to avoid refetching as session resolves.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add comprehensive per-request tracing to /debug/logs

Each webhook request now produces a single trace entry containing:
- Webhook payload (sender, message, conversation_id, inbox_id)
- Conversation history fetched from Chatwoot
- System prompt and messages sent to Anthropic
- Each Anthropic response (stop_reason, content blocks, token usage)
- Each tool execution (name, input, result)
- Final reply sent to customer
- Handoff summary if transfer occurred
- Any errors

View at /debug/logs?limit=N (default 10).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: URL spidering — crawl linked pages with configurable depth and page limits (#315) (#343) * feat: URL spidering — crawl linked pages with configurable depth and page limits (#315) Adds opt-in spidering to URL indexing. A single seed URL can now crawl and index an entire documentation site or wiki section in one call. New files: - src/core/link-extractor.ts: indexOf-based extraction, relative URL resolution, fragment stripping, dedup, scheme filtering. No regex. - src/core/spider.ts: BFS crawl engine with sameDomain, pathPrefix, excludePatterns (glob), maxPages (hard cap 200), maxDepth (hard cap 5), 10-min total timeout, robots.txt (User-agent: * and libscope), and 1s inter-request delay. Yields SpiderResult per page; returns SpiderStats. - tests/unit/link-extractor.test.ts: 25 tests covering relative resolution, dedup, fragment stripping, scheme filtering, attribute order, edge cases. - tests/unit/spider.test.ts: 20 tests covering BFS order, depth/page limits, domain + path + pattern filtering, cycle detection, robots.txt, partial failure recovery, stats, and abortReason reporting. Modified: - src/core/url-fetcher.ts: adds fetchRaw() export returning raw body + contentType + finalUrl before HTML-to-markdown conversion, so the spider can extract links from HTML before conversion. - src/api/routes.ts: POST /api/v1/documents/url now accepts spider, maxPages, maxDepth, sameDomain, pathPrefix, excludePatterns. Returns { documents, pagesIndexed, pagesCrawled, pagesSkipped, errors, abortReason }. - src/mcp/server.ts: submit-document tool gains spider, maxPages, maxDepth, sameDomain, pathPrefix, excludePatterns parameters. Safety: all fetched URLs pass through the existing SSRF validation in fetchRaw() (DNS resolution, private IP blocking, scheme allowlist). Hard limits (200 pages, depth 5, 10min) cannot be overridden by callers. robots.txt is fetched once per origin and Disallow rules are honoured. Individual page failures do not abort the crawl. Closes #315 Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * fix: resolve CI lint errors in spider implementation - Remove unnecessary type assertions (routes.ts, mcp/server.ts) — TypeScript already narrows SpiderResult/SpiderStats from the generator - Add explicit return type annotation on mock fetchRaw to satisfy no-unsafe-return rule in spider.test.ts - Replace .resolves.not.toThrow() with a direct assertion — vitest .resolves requires a Promise, not an async function Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * fix: address CodeQL security findings in spider/link-extractor link-extractor.ts (CodeQL #30 — incomplete URL scheme check): Replace the enumerated scheme blocklist (javascript:, vbscript:, etc.) with a strict http/https allowlist check on the resolved URL protocol. An allowlist is exhaustive by definition; a blocklist will always miss obscure schemes like vbscript:, blob:, or future additions. spider.ts (CodeQL #31 — incomplete multi-character sanitization): Replace the regex-based tag stripper /<[^>]+>/g in extractTitle() with an indexOf-based stripTags() function. The regex stops at the first > which can be inside a quoted attribute value (e.g. ), potentially leaving partial tag content in the extracted title. The new implementation walks quoted attribute values explicitly so no tag content leaks through regardless of its internal structure. Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * fix: address all Copilot review comments on spider PR (#343) - link-extractor: add word-boundary check in extractHref to prevent matching data-href, aria-href (false positives on non-href attributes) - spider: rename pagesIndexed → pagesFetched throughout (SpiderStats interface already used pagesFetched; sync implementation + tests) - spider: per-origin robots.txt cache (Map) fetched lazily as new origins are encountered during crawl (was seed-only before) - spider: normalize to raw.finalUrl after redirects — visited set, yielded URL, and link-extraction base all use the canonical URL - routes: validate maxPages/maxDepth are finite positive integers - routes: change conditional spread &&-patterns to ternaries - routes: remove inner try/catch for spider fetch errors; add FetchError to top-level handler (consistent with single-URL mode → 502) - mcp/server: replace conditional spreads with explicit if assignments - mcp/server: validate spider=true requires url (throws ValidationError) - openapi: document spider request fields in IndexFromUrlRequest schema, add SpiderResponse schema, update 201 response to oneOf Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * style: fix prettier formatting in spider files Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

commit.message:
Fix consumer startup race condition with Docker healthcheck and retry logic (#7)

Add healthcheck to zaza service (TCP socket probe) so consumer waits for
the server to accept connections before starting. Add exponential backoff
retry to McpClients.connect() as defense-in-depth for transient connection
failures. Declare httpx as explicit consumer dependency.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Polish review tables: confirm delete, star ratings, better empty states

- Delete buttons now require confirmation (click once → "Confirm?",
  click again → actually deletes) to prevent accidental data loss
- Ratings display as gold star glyphs (★★★★☆) instead of bare numbers
- Rating dropdown options labeled "1 — Poor" through "5 — Excellent"
- Edit mode inputs have cyan border to visually distinguish from read
- Editing row gets subtle highlight via data-editing attribute
- Action buttons use ghost style (outline) for Edit/Delete/Block,
  solid only for Save — clearer visual hierarchy

fix(auth): aumentar timeout de redirect para 2000ms no DashboardLayout

processSession do AuthProvider pode levar até 1.5s para concluir
(fetch Supabase + validação usuário ativo). O debounce anterior de
400ms causava race condition pós-login: signIn() retorna antes de
processSession completar, gerando janela breve de user=null que
disparava redirect prematuro para /login.

commit.message:
Streamline README: Focus on our contributions

Major Improvements:
==================
- Reduced from 669 to 222 lines (67% shorter!)
- Removed entire 'Original MBRDL Documentation' section (~300 lines)
- Removed verbose statistical examples (link to full docs instead)
- Clearer focus on our unique contributions

Structure:
==========
✓ Our work (MDAT, MRAT, optimizations) - prominently featured
✓ Quick start guide - concise and actionable
✓ Algorithm comparison table - easy to scan
✓ Statistical analysis - brief example with link to full docs
✓ Testing & performance - clear commands
✓ Original MBRDL - brief acknowledgment with link

Benefits:
=========
- Faster to read and understand
- Clear distinction between our work and base framework
- Appropriate credit without overshadowing our contributions
- Links to original repo for those who need base documentation
- Professional and focused presentation

Line count: 669 → 222 lines (-67%)

commit.message:
fix: address all 6 team-reported defects from v3.0 launch review

DEF-NFR-001: HSTS header now always set in production (ProxyFix added
  for Railway reverse proxy — request.is_secure was always False behind TLS proxy)

DEF-NFR-002: Brute-force lockout moved from per-worker in-memory to
  database-backed (failed_login_count, locked_until columns on users table).
  Works correctly across all 4 Gunicorn workers. 5 attempts → 15min lockout.

DEF-E2E-UI-001/002: Donor wizard upload now shows phase-specific feedback
  (saving_draft → uploading → analyzing), auto-scrolls to result, adds
  data-extraction-status attributes for automated testing, handles all
  error paths with visible UI state.

DEF-I18N-001: Replaced 40+ hardcoded English strings with T() calls.
  All 675 translation keys validated across en/ar/fr/es. Added RTL rules
  for extraction results, wizard actions, framework tabs, search inputs.

Oversized upload: Added early Content-Length check in middleware returning
  413 with clear message instead of backend 503.

Migration v301_lockout adds 3 columns to users table (idempotent).

commit.message:
fix(compact): atomic remove+inject to prevent UnboundLocalError in LangChain

Two separate aupdate_state calls (remove, then add) left the state in an
invalid intermediate with no AIMessage. LangChain's factory.py function
_fetch_last_ai_and_tool_messages assumes an AIMessage always exists and
raises UnboundLocalError when none is found.

Fix: combine RemoveMessage ops and the new summary HumanMessage into a
single aupdate_state call so the state transition is atomic.
